summary: Iteration 30 full suite PASSED. 20/20 backend pytest tests + 3/3 frontend flows (Contact, Attendance, Login footer) green. Verified: attendance CRUD + upsert + bulk + roster + delete + status/worker validation; Telegram /attendance command (headcount by name, per-worker mark, no-arg summary, multi-project picker); att_head callback records headcount; @NithinV16 chat_id auto-capture (case-insensitive) with negative test that random users do NOT populate contact_chat_id; /api/company-info returns SIXN8 legal name + admin@dukaaon.in with no CONTACT_TG_USERNAME leak; /api/contact validation, rate limit (429), successful submission, and end-to-end delivery to captured chat_id with escaped fields and no telegram_username field leak in db.contact_submissions; NithinV16 handle NEVER appears in any API response, HTML source, or JS bundle. Frontend Attendance page: roster with 3 status buttons per worker, accent-color toggling, headcount form with toast, 'Mark all present' bulk. Contact page shows SIXN8 + admin@dukaaon.in, blank-submit blocked, submission attempted (429 in test env is expected). Login footer has 'Contact us' link + SIXN8 attribution. No issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 4,053 lines — the split into routers (auth/expenses/telegram/attendance/contact/compliance) previously flagged in iter29 is more urgent as attendance and contact/webhook logic has grown.

Contact rate-limit is IP-based and in-process (single-pod). Behind a proxy where request.client.host resolves to the ingress IP, all users share one bucket. Confirm karyaai.app fronting preserves client IP via X-Forwarded-For (currently NOT read in rate_limit key).

_handle_tg_attendance_command uses find_by_name for both worker and project fuzzy matching — good UX but if a worker is named the same as a status keyword ('Present') the regex path could misroute. Low priority.
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_karya1 (user test-user-karya1, DB karya_db_v2). Unchanged.
seed_data_creation: None persistent. All test rows (attendance marks, contact_submissions with iter30qa@nowhere.example, throwaway system_config entries) are cleaned up in-test. Rate-limit tests leave the /api/contact bucket temporarily saturated — a subsequent human submission may see 429 for ~5 min.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Motor async client is bound to whatever event loop first touches it — do NOT use asyncio.run(...) or starlette TestClient for tests that also read/write via server.db (Motor). Either (a) share a single event loop across the module (see _LOOP in test_iteration30.py), or (b) use pymongo (sync_db in the same file) for DB setup/verify + `requests` against the live server for HTTP calls. The webhook secret 'karya-tg-hook-7f3a9c2e' is in backend/.env under TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET and MUST be sent as X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token. When testing /api/contact rate limiting, expect prior test runs to leave the bucket saturated — either wait 5 min or use time.sleep(60) retries as done in TestContactTelegramDelivery.
